mnemonics: CAP-V: Consider All Possibilities - Virtualize. Think about Capacity, Applications, and Performance when planning Virtualization.
keyPoints: Virtualization abstracts physical resources (CPU, memory, storage) to create multiple virtual machines (VMs) on a single host.
Cloud computing provides on-demand, scalable IT resources (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) over the internet, often built on virtualized data centers.
Effective capacity planning for virtual/cloud environments requires monitoring utilization, forecasting demand, and understanding scalability options (vertical/horizontal).
commonMistakes: Underestimating the 'noisy neighbor' effect, where one VM consumes excessive resources and degrades performance for others on the same host.
Failing to plan for cloud cost management, leading to budget overruns from unused resources or over-provisioning.
